# supabase_auth_settings (Resource)

Auth settings resource for a Supabase project.



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `project_ref` (String) Project reference ID.

### Optional

- `disable_signup` (Boolean) Disable new user sign-ups.
- `external` (Attributes) OAuth provider configuration. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--external))
- `external_email_enabled` (Boolean) Allow email sign-ins.
- `external_phone_enabled` (Boolean) Allow phone sign-ins.
- `hooks` (Attributes) Auth hook configuration. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--hooks))
- `jwt_exp` (Number) JWT expiry in seconds.
- `mfa` (Attributes) Multi-factor authentication settings. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mfa))
- `password_min_length` (Number) Minimum password length.
- `security` (Attributes) Security settings. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--security))
- `sessions` (Attributes) Session settings. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--sessions))
- `site_url` (String) The base URL of the site.
- `smtp` (Attributes) Custom SMTP server settings. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--smtp))
- `timeouts` (Block, Optional) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--timeouts))

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Resource identifier (same as project_ref).
- `secret_hashes` (Map of String) API-returned hashes for sensitive fields, keyed by API field name. Used for out-of-band drift detection.

<a id="nestedatt--mfa"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mfa`

Optional:

- `max_enrolled_factors` (Number) Maximum number of enrolled MFA factors per user.
- `phone` (Attributes) Phone MFA factor settings. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mfa--phone))
- `totp` (Attributes) TOTP factor settings. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mfa--totp))
- `web_authn` (Attributes) WebAuthn factor settings. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mfa--web_authn))

<a id="nestedatt--mfa--phone"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mfa.phone`

Optional:

- `enroll_enabled` (Boolean)
- `max_frequency` (Number) Minimum interval between MFA challenge requests (seconds).
- `otp_length` (Number) Length of the OTP code.
- `template` (String) SMS template for MFA OTP.
- `verify_enabled` (Boolean)


<a id="nestedatt--mfa--totp"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mfa.totp`

Optional:

- `enroll_enabled` (Boolean)
- `verify_enabled` (Boolean)


<a id="nestedatt--mfa--web_authn"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mfa.web_authn`

Optional:

- `enroll_enabled` (Boolean)
- `verify_enabled` (Boolean)



<a id="nestedatt--security"></a>
### Nested Schema for `security`

Optional:

- `captcha_enabled` (Boolean) Enable CAPTCHA protection.
- `captcha_provider` (String) CAPTCHA provider: `hcaptcha` or `turnstile`.
- `captcha_secret` (String, Sensitive) CAPTCHA secret key.
- `manual_linking_enabled` (Boolean) Enable manual account linking.


<a id="nestedatt--sessions"></a>
### Nested Schema for `sessions`

Optional:

- `inactivity_timeout` (Number) Inactivity timeout (seconds). 0 means unlimited.
- `single_per_user` (Boolean) Allow only one active session per user.
- `timebox` (Number) Maximum session duration (seconds). 0 means unlimited.


<a id="nestedatt--smtp"></a>
### Nested Schema for `smtp`

Optional:

- `admin_email` (String) Sender email address.
- `host` (String) SMTP host.
- `max_frequency` (Number) Minimum interval between emails sent to the same address (seconds).
- `pass` (String, Sensitive) SMTP password.
- `port` (String) SMTP port.
- `sender_name` (String) Display name for the sender.
- `user` (String) SMTP username.


<a id="nestedblock--timeouts"></a>
### Nested Schema for `timeouts`

Optional:

- `create` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).
- `update` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).
